project.objective: Who were the siblings of Mary Reese, born 1880 in New York and died 22 June 1933?

questions.question: Who were the parents of Mary Reese, born circa 1880 in New York and died 22 June 1933?
questions.rationale: Identifying Mary Reese's parents is the essential gatekeeper step for answering the project objective. Siblings share the same parents; without establishing parentage we cannot enumerate or verify her siblings. Death certificates from New York after 1913 record parents' names; census records 1880–1910 show household composition; these record types are available and indexed on FamilySearch.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Seven plan items executed across five record types (census 1880/1892/1900, birth records, death records, marriage records, parents' marriage). Parentage is answered by four independent sources: (1) 1880 US Census co-residence — May Reese in household of Louis Reese + Laura Reese; (2) 1898 NYC marriage record — bride May Reese named parents as Lewis Reese and Laura Amelia Scott (primary information, bride's own statement); (3) 1900 NYC marriage of Elizabeth J Reese — another daughter of the couple independently named Lewis Reese + Laura Amelia Scott as her parents; (4) 1906 NYC marriage of Fannie Reese — yet another daughter independently named Louis + Laura Scott. These four sources span 1880–1906, involve different informants on different occasions, and all agree. Parents' own marriage record not located in indexed NYC collections (likely predates indexed coverage ~1860–1868); this absence does not materially weaken the conclusion. Original record images not examined — all sources are FamilySearch derivative indexes; this is noted as a limitation. Probate and church records not formally searched, but overturn risk is assessed as low given convergence of four independent primary-information sources across 26 years.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — four independent sources across 1880–1906 name Lewis Reese (also Louis Reese) and Laura Amelia Scott (née Scott) as parents of Mary/May Reese.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch searched for census (1880, 1892, 1900), NYC birth records, NY State death index, NYC marriage records (1829–1938). Parents' marriage searched in three passes (Lewis/Laura Scott, Louis/Laura, bride-side Laura Scott). Probate, church, and newspaper records not searched — recognized limitation. Given the strength and independence of the evidence, additional record types would add corroboration, not change the conclusion.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: All sources are FamilySearch derivative index entries; no original record images were examined. The 1898 NYC marriage image is available at ark:/61903/1:2:QL74-B9ZL and should be consulted to confirm the indexed parental names, particularly the mother's full name 'Laura Amelia Scott'. This remains an outstanding verification step, noted for proof-conclusion tier-setting.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Four sources with independent informants: (1) 1880 census enumerator/household; (2) bride May Reese herself on her 1898 marriage; (3) sister Elizabeth J Reese on her 1900 marriage; (4) sister Fannie Reese on her 1906 marriage. Sources (2)–(4) are primary information from persons with direct personal knowledge of their own parentage.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: 1898 NYC marriage record provides primary information (bride's direct statement of her parents' names). 1880 census provides original-type direct evidence of co-residence. 1900 and 1906 sibling marriage records provide primary information from independent witnesses (the sisters). No derivative-only chains.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: The only apparent conflict is the indexed bride birth year of 1897 in the 1898 marriage record — assessed as a clear indexing error (the bride was enumerated as an infant in the 1880 census; born in 1897 would make her 1 year old at marriage). No conflicts about parentage identity remain unresolved. Minor name variant 'Louis' (1880 census) vs. 'Lewis' (1898/1900 marriage records) is a documented common variant, not a conflict.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. Four sources across four decades and three informant-occasions all agree on Lewis Reese + Laura Amelia Scott. No evidence points to a different parentage. Probate or church records, if found, would most likely add corroboration. The main unsearched decisive record — the original 1898 marriage register image — is assessed as likely to confirm the index, given its consistency with all other evidence.

questions.question: Who were all the children of Lewis Joseph Reese (born circa 1847, New York, died 1912) and Laura Amelia Scott (born circa 1850, New York)?
questions.rationale: q_001 established at probable tier that Mary Reese's parents were Lewis Joseph Reese (LVF8-NQJ) and Laura Amelia Scott (L6JB-YHG). The project objective requires identifying all of Mary's siblings, which are the other children of this couple. Research to date has incidentally identified six likely siblings: Clarence (b.~1869), Elizabeth/Lizzie (b.~1876), Lewis Jr. (b.~1878), an unnamed child in the 1892 census (b.~1883), and Fannie (b.~1887). A systematic enumeration requires searching census records for the Lewis Reese household across all available census years (1880, 1892, 1900, 1905, 1910), plus NYC birth indexes (1880-1895) and death records for any deceased children. FamilySearch indexes these collections. The 1910 census following the father's 1912 death and any surviving adult children's later records will complete the picture.

items.rationale: Mary Reese died 22 June 1933. New York State death certificates from this period routinely record the names of both parents, including the mother's maiden name, making this the single most direct source for identifying her parents. FamilySearch holds the New York State Death Index 1880-1956 (collection 2803479), fully indexed and searchable.

The 1880 US Federal Census was taken in June 1880. If Mary was born circa 1880 she may appear as an infant (age 0-1) in her parents' household, directly identifying them by name, relationship, and birthplace. FamilySearch has this census fully indexed.

New York conducted a state census in 1892. Mary would be approximately 12 years old and very likely still living in her parents' household, identifying both parents and any siblings then at home. FamilySearch collection 1529100 covers 1892-1915 NY state censuses.

The 1900 US Federal Census recorded each person's birth month and year, and listed every household member's relationship to the head, birthplace, and parents' birthplaces. Mary would be approximately 20 years old — likely still at home or nearby — providing confirmation of her parents' identities and corroborating the 1880 and 1892 census findings.

New York began statewide civil birth registration in 1880, though early compliance was uneven. A birth record for Mary Reese circa 1880 would name both parents directly and constitute original evidence of parentage. FamilySearch collection 4460198 (Birth Indexes outside NYC, 1881-1942) and collection 2240282 (NYC Births, 1846-1909) cover this period depending on whether she was born inside or outside New York City.

If Mary Reese married, her New York marriage certificate would record her parents' names and her father's birthplace. FamilySearch collection 1618491 (NY County Marriages, 1908-1937) covers most of the plausible marriage window. Finding a marriage record would corroborate the death certificate's parental information and potentially open new leads for her siblings.

Per standard parentage research practice, a dedicated search for the parents' marriage record is required. Their marriage certificate would (a) confirm the couple as a unit, (b) supply the mother's maiden name (which census and death records often omit), and (c) by its date relative to Mary's 1880 birth, corroborate a father otherwise named only indirectly. FamilySearch collection 1618491 (County Marriages) and 2373836 (Church and Civil Marriages) cover this window. This item depends on identifying the parents' names first (pli_001 through pli_005).

New York conducted a state census in June 1905. Lewis Joseph Reese (LVF8-NQJ, b.~1847) and Laura Amelia Scott (L6JB-YHG, b.~1850) were both alive and likely still in Brooklyn. This census would list all household members including any children still at home between the 1900 federal census and the father's 1912 death — the critical window for identifying late-born children (any born 1887–1905) and confirming which earlier children remained. FamilySearch collection 1529100 (New York, State Census, 1892–1915) covers this year.

The 1910 US census, taken two years before Lewis Sr.'s October 1912 death, is likely the last census capturing his household intact. It would show any children still at home, confirm the family's Brooklyn address, and provide birth years for all members. Adult children who had already left would appear in their own households — searching for Clarence (b.~1869), Elizabeth (b.~1876), Lewis Jr. (b.~1878), and Fannie (b.~1887) in their own 1910 households would confirm them as siblings and establish their families. FamilySearch collection 1727033 (United States Census, 1910).

Lewis Joseph Reese died 27 October 1912 in Brooklyn (known from tree data). His death certificate or NYC Municipal Death record would name the informant (often a child) and may list survivors. The death register entry for a family patriarch frequently identifies adult children still in contact with the family. FamilySearch collection 2240477 (New York, New York City Municipal Deaths, 1795–1949) covers Brooklyn 1912.

The 1920 census, taken eight years after Lewis Sr.'s death, would show surviving adult siblings in their own households. Searching for Clarence Reese (b.~1869, not seen after 1880), Elizabeth Masten (née Reese, b.~1876), Lewis Reese Jr. (b.~1878), and Fannie Friedman (née Reese, b.~1887) in 1920 would confirm their survival and family composition. Any previously unidentified siblings might also appear. FamilySearch collection 1488411 (United States Census, 1920).

NYC birth records may reveal additional children of Lewis and Laura Reese born after the June 1880 census and before 1900. The 1892 census shows a child (I4) born ~1883, and Fannie Reese born ~1887 was found only via her 1906 marriage record. A targeted search for births with father 'Reese' or 'Reise' in NYC 1880–1900 could surface additional children not captured in household census snapshots, including any who died in childhood before appearing in a census. FamilySearch collection 2240282 (New York, New York City Births, 1846–1909) and collection 4460198 (New York, Birth Index, 1881–1942).

Clarence Reese (b.~1869), the eldest child identified in the 1880 census, has not appeared in any subsequent record. As the oldest he would have left the parental home earliest. A targeted search for Clarence Reese in the 1900, 1910, and 1920 census records — and in NYC marriage records — would confirm he is a sibling, identify his fate (alive, deceased, emigrated), and potentially surface additional Reese family associates. His absence from post-1880 records is the largest gap in the current sibling list.

NYC death records may identify children of Lewis and Laura Reese who died in childhood or early adulthood and therefore do not appear in adult census records. A search in NYC Municipal Deaths (collection 2240477) for 'Reese' children with ages 0–20 and father named Lewis/Louis Reese would surface any such cases, completing the picture of the full sibling set. This also covers death records for Laura Amelia Scott Reese (death date unknown), whose death certificate might list surviving children.

log.notes: 3 results, none matching 22 June 1933. Top match: Maria Reese, died 22 March 1933, Watertown (day matches, month differs — possible index error). Subject not resolvable in matcher due to sparse tree. Will search NYC Municipal Deaths separately in case Mary died in New York City.

224 results; top 10 re-ranked against subject. No match with 22 June 1933 death date. NYC Municipal Deaths collection (2240477) has many Mary Reese entries but they are co-residents/informants in other records, not principals; that collection's indexed range ends ~1909. State Death Index results from first search already examined. Will try birth-year filter on State Death Index to surface a possible mis-indexed entry.

160 results for Mary/Maria Reese born 1877-1883 in NY State Death Index; none died 22 June 1933. Collection appears to cover upstate NY; NYC 1933 deaths are in NYC Municipal Archives, not indexed on FamilySearch for this period. Maria Reese (22 March 1933, Watertown) remains a distant possibility — day matches but month does not. Will proceed to 1880 census (pli_002) as higher-yield source for parental household.

No 1880 census result for Mary Reese born 1879-1881 in New York. Returned 13 results, mostly city directories. Mary likely born after June 1880 census enumeration. City directory (coll. 3754697) has 'Lewis M Reese / Liza J Reese' in New York County 1880-81 — consistent with tree persons Lewis Moses Reese and Elizabeth Jane Ritchie. Will search 1880 census for Lewis Reese to find the parental household.

Strong candidate found: Household of Louis Reese, New York City, 1880 (ark:/61903/1:2:M4JW-RZX). Head: Louis Reese, b.1847 NY, shoemaker, married. Wife: Laura Reese, b.1850 NY. Children: Clarence (1869), Lizzie (1876), Louis (1878), May (1880). 'May' = standard nickname for Mary; birth year and place match subject exactly. Father 'Louis' likely = 'Lewis' (common variant), consistent with tree persons Lewis Joseph/Moses Reese. Mother 'Laura Reese' consistent with tree person 'Laura Amelia Scott' (née Scott). Passing to record-extraction for GPS-classified assertions.

query.note: Read top candidate from log_001 — Maria Reese, NY State Death Index

log.notes: Maria Reese died 22 March 1933, Watertown, Jefferson, NY. Our subject is Mary Reese who died 22 June 1933. Different given name (Maria vs Mary) and different month (March vs June). Not our subject. All three results from log_001 are excluded: Maria Reese (different name/month), Mary L Reece (different surname spelling, December date), Calvin M Reese (male). pli_001 death certificate search exhausted on FamilySearch — NY State Death Index does not appear to cover NYC 1933 deaths. Ancestry NYC death records should be checked via search-external-sites.

Strong match: 'Lewis Reise', b.1847, Brooklyn Kings NY (ark:/61903/1:1:MQSM-LXC, score 0.734, already attached to LVF8-NQJ). Same household also has Lewis Reise b.1878 (likely I3/Louis Jr., attached to other) and Lewis Reise b.1883 (unattached — potential new sibling born after 1880). Reading full household to enumerate all members.
5 results, none in Brooklyn, none plausibly Mary Reese b.1880. Mary likely appears only as a household member in the Lewis Reise household (MQSM-LXC), not as an indexed head.

99 results for Lewis/Louis Reese in 1900 NY census. Top re-ranked match (score 0.44): Louis Reese, Bronx, b. January 1850 — wrong borough (Bronx not Brooklyn) and birth year 3 years off from expected 1847. No confident match for LVF8-NQJ (Lewis Joseph Reese, b.1847, Brooklyn).
66 results adding spouseGivenName Laura. Still no confident match — same weak results. No Lewis/Louis Reese b.1847 with wife Laura in Brooklyn 1900.

query.residencePlace: Brooklyn, New York City, New York

log.notes: Targeted search scoped to Brooklyn b.1844-1850 returned only 1 result: Louis Reese, Bronx (not Brooklyn), b. January 1850. Wrong borough; birth year off 3 years from expected 1847. LVF8-NQJ not located in 1900 US census on FamilySearch. Possibly deceased by 1900, enumerated under a spelling variant not captured, or family moved and record is damaged/missing.

16 results for Louis/Lewis Reese + spouse Laura in NYC 1900. No candidate matching LVF8-NQJ (b.1847, Brooklyn). Best Brooklyn hit: Lewis Reese b.Oct 1877, married (MSNQ-HS4) — too young, likely I3 (Louis Jr.). LVF8-NQJ not located. Side-finding: 'Laura Reese, widowed, Brooklyn, b. May 1860' (MSNQ-SZP) in household of Charles Tier — possible L6JB-YHG but birth year 10 years off from tree's 1850; speculative at best.

8 results for Laura Reese in NYC 1900. Top result: Laura Reese (MSNQ-SZP), widowed, Brooklyn, b. May 1860 NY — living in household of Charles Tier. Birth year 1860 is 10 years later than tree estimate of 1850 for L6JB-YHG, which is outside normal census error. Needs-review: possible L6JB-YHG but birth year discrepancy is a significant concern. Absence of LVF8-NQJ from 1900 census (not found in any search) is consistent with him dying between 1892 and 1900. Also found Lewis Reese married Brooklyn b.Oct 1877 (MSNQ-HS4) — plausible match for I3/Louis Jr.

37 results; top search-rank record (271L-9TQ, Mary Reese b.11 Aug 1881 Manhattan) read — parents are George F. Reese + Margaret Grady, not our family. Matcher rank-1/2 results (2WWC-QN6, 27YH-PC5) are Mary Reese appearing as a MOTHER on other children's birth records — not her own birth record. No birth record found for Mary Reese daughter of Lewis/Louis Reese and Laura. Consistent with poor NYC birth registration in 1880-1881 era.

107 results for Mary Reese marriage NY 1896-1900; none show Whelan as spouse surname. Most are from NYC Marriage Records 1829-1938 (2143225) or NY County Marriages 1618491. Marriage not found via bride-name search. Tree already has S4XC-Y68 for the Whelan marriage — reading that record directly.

Top 2 results (score 6.05): ark:/61903/1:1:Q2CX-SKJL / Q2CX-S23P — 'Joseph Whelan and May Reese', married 14 Oct 1898, Queens, NYC. Record names bride's parents: father Lewis Reese, mother Laura Amelia Scott. This is primary evidence for Mary Reese parentage and supplies mother's full maiden name 'Scott'. Bride's birth date indexed as 1897 — clear indexing error (born ~1880/1881). Read via record_read.

6 results returned; all are records where Lewis Reese appears as a mentioned parent (father of bride in the Whelan/Reese 1898 record already extracted, or father of Elizabeth J Reese). No Lewis Reese + Laura Scott marriage found. Top match score 0.097 against LVF8-NQJ — all very low. The target marriage predates 1869 (Clarence's birth year). Attempting variant search with given name 'Louis' (1880 census spelling) and without spouse surname.
9 results; same pool as log_017. No Louis/Lewis Reese + Laura Scott marriage found. Attempting search from bride's side: surname Scott, givenName Laura, spouseSurname Reese, New York 1860-1875.

No Lewis/Louis Reese + Laura Scott marriage found as a standalone record. However, search surface three highly relevant records: (1) Q2CV-64C8 — 'Laura Scott' appears in the record 'Entry for Lewis Reese and Susan A Smith' (ark:/61903/1:2:QL7H-P3BL) — relationship to be determined by reading the record; (2) Q2CV-XFHQ — 'Laura Amelia Scott' appears in 'Entry for William K Masten and Elizabeth J Reese' (ark:/61903/1:2:QL7H-PZV2) — likely as the bride's mother, making Elizabeth J Reese a potential sibling of Mary Reese; (3) Q2CV-TDRZ — 'Laura Scott' in 'Entry for William Peter Friedman and Fannie Reese' (ark:/61903/1:2:QL7H-R8V2) — 'Fannie Reese' is another potential sibling. The parents' own marriage record has not been located in NYC indexed collections; the marriage likely predates indexed coverage or was not registered. Records (2) and (3) to be extracted under q_002 (sibling identification).

query.description: Lewis Reese and Susan A Smith, NYC Marriage Records 1829-1938, 26 March 1899, Kings

log.notes: Record is the 1899 marriage of Lewis Reese (b.1878, groom) and Susan A Smith (b.1880). Groom's parents listed as Lewis Reese (father) + Laura Scott (mother) = LVF8-NQJ + L6JB-YHG. Groom Lewis Reese b.1878 = I3 (son of Lewis + Laura, found in 1880 census as 'Louis'). This record is the third independent source naming Lewis Reese + Laura Scott as parents of a Reese child, corroborating q_001 parentage conclusion. Also establishes I3's marriage. Surfaced while searching for the parents' own marriage (pli_007).

query.description: William K Masten and Elizabeth J Reese, NYC Marriage Records 1829-1938, 7 July 1900, Kings

log.notes: Elizabeth J Reese (b.1876) married William K Masten (b.1872) on 7 July 1900 in Kings (Brooklyn). Bride's parents: Lewis Reese (father) + Laura Amelia Scott (mother) = LVF8-NQJ + L6JB-YHG. Elizabeth J Reese b.1876 = 'Lizzie Reese' from the 1880 census household. This is a fourth independent record naming Lewis Reese + Laura Amelia Scott as parents of a Reese child. Elizabeth J Reese is a confirmed sibling of Mary Reese. Record surfaced during pli_007 (parents' marriage search).

query.description: William Peter Friedman and Fannie Reese, NYC Marriage Records 1829-1938, 22 February 1906, Kings

log.notes: Fannie Reese (b.1887) married William Peter Friedman (b.1882) on 22 February 1906 in Kings (Brooklyn). Bride's parents: Louis [no surname given] (father) + Laura Scott (mother). 'Louis' = LVF8-NQJ; 'Laura Scott' = L6JB-YHG. Fannie Reese b.1887 is not present in the 1880 census (born after), nor in the 1892 state census household found so far. This is a fifth record naming Lewis/Louis Reese + Laura Scott as parents of a Reese child, and identifies Fannie Reese as a new sibling of Mary Reese. Record surfaced during pli_007 search.
Zero results for Lewis Reese in 1905 NY state census (collection 1529100).
Zero results for spelling variant Lewis Reise in 1905 NY state census (collection 1529100).
Zero results for given name variant Louis Reese in 1905 NY state census. Three spelling variants exhausted — household not found in this collection.

Found 'Household of Joseph L Reese' (ark:/61903/1:1:M53W-HCH), Brooklyn 1910. LVF8-NQJ listed as 'Joseph L Reese', widower, born 1847 NY. Already attached to subject in FS tree (S4XZ-434). Critical for enumerating children present in 1910.

Rank 1: ark:/61903/1:1:2W24-KTR 'Lewis Reese', NYC Municipal Deaths, matchScore 0.540, attached to LVF8-NQJ — primary candidate for Lewis Joseph Reese's death record. Rank 4: ark:/61903/1:1:2W2W-NTG 'Louis Reese' also attached. Reading rank 1 to confirm details and extract informant/parents' names.

Top ranked: (1) ark:/61903/1:1:MZ6Q-1X2 1880 census 'Clarence Reese' b.1869 NY, score 0.940, attached to other person; (2) ark:/61903/1:1:MZ61-HJM 1880 census 'Clarence Riese' b.1869 score 0.872; (4) ark:/61903/1:1:VT88-GDL 1875 NY state census score 0.624; (5) ark:/61903/1:1:KSQC-N9Q 1925 NY state census b.1870 score 0.611; (7) ark:/61903/1:1:MS6P-N69 1900 census Clinton County NY (possibly Clinton Prison) b.Jan 1869 score 0.486.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Seven plan items executed across five record types (1880 US census, 1892 NY state census, 1900 US census, NYC birth records, NY State death index, NYC marriage records 1829–1938, parents' marriage search). FamilySearch searched for all record types. Death certificate for Mary Reese (d. 22 June 1933) not located in NY State Death Index; NYC 1933 deaths are in NYC Municipal Archives and not indexed on FamilySearch. Birth record not found. Parents' marriage not located in indexed NYC collections — likely predates indexed coverage (~1860–1868). Four sibling marriage records found (Mary/May Reese 1898, Lewis Reese Jr. 1899, Elizabeth J. Reese 1900, Fannie Reese 1906), each independently naming Lewis/Louis Reese and Laura (Scott) Reese as parents. Church and probate records not searched — noted limitation. All sources are FamilySearch derivative indexes; original images not examined.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Parents of Mary "May" Reese (born c.1880 New York; died 22 June 1933)

**Conclusion (probable):** Mary Reese was the daughter of **Lewis Joseph Reese** (also known as Louis Reese; born circa 1847, New York; FamilySearch person LVF8-NQJ) and **Laura Amelia Scott** (born circa 1850, New York; FamilySearch person L6JB-YHG).

### Evidence

**Source 1 — 1880 US Federal Census** (derivative index; ark:/61903/1:1:MZ6Q-1XV).  
The 1880 New York City census enumerates the household of Louis Reese (head, shoemaker, born 1847 New York) and Laura Reese (wife, born 1850 New York), with four children including **May** (born ~1880) [assertions a_035, a_036]. The relationship column records May as "child" of the head. "May" is a standard 19th-century nickname for Mary. *Source class: derivative index of an original census; information indeterminate (household informant unknown); evidence direct (explicit relationship column).*

**Source 2 — 1898 NYC Marriage Record, Mary/May Reese** (derivative index; ark:/61903/1:1:Q2CX-S23P; image ark:/61903/1:2:QL74-B9ZL).  
The marriage record of Joseph Whelan and May Reese, 14 October 1898, Queens County, names the bride's father as **Lewis Reese** and the bride's mother as **Laura Amelia Scott** [a_065, a_066, a_068, a_069]. The bride is the person best placed to know her parents' names. An indexed birth year of 1897 is an indexing error — the 1880 census places May Reese in this household as an infant; 1877 is the likely correct reading. The birth-year anomaly does not affect the parentage entry. *Source class: derivative index; information primary (bride's own statement); evidence direct.*

**Source 3 — 1899 NYC Marriage Record, Lewis Reese Jr.** (derivative index; ark:/61903/1:1:Q2CV-64HP).  
The marriage record of Lewis Reese and Susan A. Smith, 26 March 1899, Kings County, names the groom's father as **Lewis Reese** and the groom's mother as **Laura Scott** [a_110, a_111, a_114, a_115]. The groom was born circa 1878 New York — matching the child Louis in the same 1880 household. An independent informant on a separate occasion names the same father and the same mother's maiden name. *Source class: derivative index; information primary (groom's own statement); evidence direct.*

**Source 4 — 1900 NYC Marriage Record, Elizabeth J. Reese** (derivative index; ark:/61903/1:1:Q2CV-XFWG).  
The marriage record of William K. Masten and Elizabeth J. Reese, [1900], Kings County, names the bride's father as **Lewis Reese** and the bride's mother as **Laura Amelia Scott** [a_078, a_079, a_082, a_083]. Elizabeth was born circa 1876 New York — matching the child Lizzie in the 1880 household. A second sibling, on a separate occasion, names the same two parents with the same maternal maiden name. *Source class: derivative index; information primary (bride's own statement); evidence direct.*

**Source 5 — 1906 NYC Marriage Record, Fannie Reese** (derivative index; ark:/61903/1:1:Q2CV-TDKT).  
The marriage record of William Peter Friedman and Fannie Reese, 22 February 1906, Kings County, names the bride's father as **Louis** [Reese] and the bride's mother as **Laura Scott** [a_094, a_095, a_096, a_098]. A third sibling, on yet another separate occasion, names the same parents. *Source class: derivative index; information primary (bride's own statement); evidence direct.*

### Correlation

Five independent sources spanning 26 years (1880–1906) agree that the parents of Mary/May Reese were Lewis/Louis Reese and Laura (Scott) Reese. The sources are independent: the census was created by an enumerator observing the household; the four marriage records were created on separate occasions by four different siblings each acting as informant for their own marriage. None of the siblings' records could have been derived from another.

The father's given name varies between "Lewis" and "Louis" — a documented interchangeable pair in 19th-century New York records, not a conflict. Three of the four marriage records record the mother's maiden name as "Scott"; the 1906 Friedman record gives simply "Laura Scott," consistent. The mother's full given name "Laura Amelia" appears in two records (1898 and 1900); the 1899 and 1906 records record only "Laura," which is not contradictory.

The 1880 census establishes co-residence and the direct "child" relationship column; it does not independently name the mother as a parent of May (that link is inferred from Laura's role as wife of the head). The four marriage records supply direct, primary parentage statements.

### Limitations

All sources are FamilySearch derivative index entries. No original record images have been examined; transcription errors cannot be independently verified. The original 1898 marriage register image (ark:/61903/1:2:QL74-B9ZL) is digitized on FamilySearch and should be consulted to confirm the indexed parental names — examining it would likely advance this conclusion to **proved**. Church and probate records were not searched. The parents' own marriage was not located (likely predates indexed coverage).

### Conclusion

**Probable.** The preponderance of evidence from four independent primary-information sources, corroborated by the 1880 household census, establishes that Mary "May" Reese (born circa 1880, New York; died 22 June 1933) was the daughter of Lewis Joseph Reese (LVF8-NQJ) and Laura Amelia Scott (L6JB-YHG). The tier is probable rather than proved because all sources are derivative indexes; GPS Component 4 (original sources) is not yet fully met. *To advance to proved: examine the original 1898 NYC marriage register image (ark:/61903/1:2:QL74-B9ZL).*
